name: Niger (Republic of Niger / République du Niger)
summary: Niger is a West African Sahel mixed-legal-system republic combining French civil-law substantive heritage (via colonial inheritance) with Maliki Islamic-law personal-status provisions and customary-law jurisdiction (largely uncodified). Niger is structurally distinctive within the corpus as one of the few states without a comprehensive Family or Persons and Family Code — family-law operates through dispersed statutes (Marriage Ordinance, Custom Codification regulations) supplemented heavily by uncodified customary-law and Maliki Islamic-law for relevant communities. Parental authority and child custody are governed by case-law and the Civil Code of 1804 (Napoleonic, residual application). The Court of Cassation (Cour de Cassation) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters; the Constitutional Court (Cour Constitutionnelle) operates separate constitutional review. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Court of First Instance (Tribunal de Grande Instance), customary courts, and Islamic-law tribunals (Tribunaux des Affaires Coutumières). Psychology profession is regulated through the Ministry of Health framework. Niger is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the interest-of-the-child standard. Niger is non-Hague Convention.

statutory_framework.title: Civil Code (Napoleonic, residual)
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Civil Code drawing on French Napoleonic Code substantive heritage; applied residually where dispersed family-law statutes do not address.

statutory_framework.title: Custom Codification framework
statutory_framework.relevance: Regulatory framework codifying selected customary-law personal-status provisions; supplemented by uncodified customary-law applied by Tribunaux des Affaires Coutumières.

apex_courts.name: Court of Cassation (Cour de Cassation)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Constitutional Court (Cour Constitutionnelle)
apex_courts.role: Constitutional Court with original jurisdiction over constitutional review.

professional_regulators.name: Ministry of Public Health, Population and Social Affairs, Niger
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Nigerien family-court decisions are anonymised per Court of Cassation practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Niger independence + Hamani Diori founding-presidency
key_developments.description: Niger independence 3 August 1960 from France — Hamani Diori first president 1960-1974 substantively distinctive Sahel founding-presidency framework. Foundational pre-1974-coup framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: 1974 Niger coup + Kountché 13-year-military-rule framework
key_developments.description: 1974 Niger coup d'état 15 April 1974 led by Colonel Seyni Kountché ending Diori presidency — substantively distinctive Sahel recurring-coup framework. Subsequent 13-year-military-rule 1974-1987 substantially affecting political-institutional framework persisting through subsequent democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Niger ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: Niger ratified the UNCRC on 30 September 1990 (with reservations consistent with Islamic Sharia) — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration applied through Civil Code residual + customary-law + Maliki Islamic-law framework.

key_developments.title: Multi-party transition + Constitution + democratic-trajectory framework
key_developments.description: Multi-party transition 1991-1993 — Constitution of Niger 1992 adopted by referendum substantively reforming political-institutional framework. Substantively significant Sahel democratic-transition framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Custom Codification regulations + customary-law-framework consolidation
key_developments.description: Regulatory framework codifying selected customary-law personal-status provisions — substantively distinctive Sahel customary-law-codification framework. Foundational pre-2010 customary-law framework supplemented by uncodified customary-law applied by Tribunaux des Affaires Coutumières.

key_developments.title: 1996 Niger coup + Maïnassara + recurring-coup-pattern framework
key_developments.description: 1996 Niger coup 27 January 1996 led by Colonel Ibrahim Baré Maïnassara substantively disrupting political-institutional framework. Subsequent 1999 coup + Maïnassara assassination substantively distinctive Sahel recurring-coup pattern affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: 2010 Niger coup + Constitution 2010 + Seventh-Republic framework
key_developments.description: 2010 Niger coup 18 February 2010 ending Tandja presidency. Constitution of Niger 2010 adopted 31 October 2010 by referendum substantively reforming political-institutional framework establishing Seventh Republic. Substantive constitutional anchor for contemporary family-law jurisprudence.

key_developments.title: Niger-Mali border-conflict + Tuareg-rebellion-context + Sahel-armed-conflict
key_developments.description: Niger-Mali border substantively affected by Mali-Tuareg-rebellion 2012-onwards. Subsequent Boko Haram cross-border-armed-conflict in southeast Niger from 2015. Substantive Sahel armed-conflict framework substantially affecting demographic and family-law-implementation framework.

key_developments.title: 2023 Niger coup + Tchiani + post-Western-disengagement-Russian-Wagner-realignment framework
key_developments.description: 2023 Niger coup 26 July 2023 led by General Abdourahamane Tchiani ending Bazoum presidency. Substantively distinctive Sahel post-Western-disengagement-Russian-Wagner-realignment framework (alongside Mali 2020, Burkina Faso 2022) — French withdrawal from Niger December 2023. Substantive cross-border-jurisdiction-practice framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Niger ECOWAS withdrawal + AES framework joining + Russian-Wagner alignment
key_developments.description: Niger (with Mali, Burkina Faso) ECOWAS withdrawal announcement January 2024 (effective January 2025) following 2023 ECOWAS sanctions and threatened military intervention. Niger joining Alliance des États du Sahel (AES) September 2023. Russian-Wagner Group (subsequently Africa Corps) alignment substantively reshaping geopolitical framework. Substantively significant Sahel institutional context affecting subsequent cross-border family-law adjudication.

key_developments.title: Cour de Cassation + Cour Constitutionnelle — interest-of-the-child substantive register + Tchiani-transitional framework
key_developments.description: Cour de Cassation and Cour Constitutionnelle continue to develop interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Civil Code 1804 residual + Custom Codification regulations + Maliki Islamic-law framework via Tribunaux des Affaires Coutumières + Islamic-law tribunals in custody disputes within Tchiani transitional-government framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Niger operates a structurally distinctive mixed-legal-system framework — French civil-law (Civil Code 1804 residual) + Maliki Islamic-law personal-status + uncodified customary-law via Tribunaux des Affaires Coutumières. Among the few states without a comprehensive Family Code or Persons and Family Code in the Sahel cluster.
Absence of comprehensive Family Code is structurally distinctive within the Sahel Francophone cluster — Mali (2011), Burkina Faso (1989), Senegal (1972) all have comprehensive Family Codes.
Non-Hague Convention status places Niger in the non-Hague Sahel West African cluster.
editorial_notes: Niger jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 3 to 10 key_developments with full Diori-to-Tchiani trajectory: 1960-Niger-independence-+-Hamani-Diori + 1974-1974-Niger-coup-+-Kountché-13-year-military-rule + 1990-UNCRC-ratification + 1993-Multi-party-transition-+-Constitution + 1995-Custom-Codification-regulations + 1996-1996-Niger-coup-+-Maïnassara + 2010-2010-Niger-coup-+-Constitution-+-Seventh-Republic + 2012-Niger-Mali-border-conflict-+-Tuareg-+-Boko-Haram + 2023-2023-Niger-coup-+-Tchiani-+-post-Western-disengagement + 2024-Cour-de-Cassation-+-Cour-Constitutionnelle-+-Tchiani-transitional.

Mixed-legal-system Sahel Francophone West Africa (Civil Code 1804 residual + Maliki Islamic-law + customary-law via Tribunaux des Affaires Coutumières + non-Hague). Absence of comprehensive Family Code distinctive within Sahel.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive interest-of-the-child analysis under Civil Code 1804 residual + Custom Codification + Maliki Islamic-law framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Sahel-Francophone-West-African + mixed-legal-system + no-comprehensive-Family-Code-distinctive (with Chad) + recurring-coup-pattern-1974-1996-1999-2010-2023 + Kountché-13-year-military-1974-1987 + 2023-Niger-coup-+-Tchiani-+-post-Western-disengagement-Russian-Wagner-realignment (with Mali 2020, Burkina Faso 2022) + French-withdrawal-December-2023 + Boko-Haram-cross-border-armed-conflict-southeast-Niger + non-Hague-Convention clusters within the corpus.
